stem: An angle measures $35°$. What type of angle is this?
hint: An acute angle is less than $90°$.
explanation: $35°$ is between $0°$ and $90°$, so it is an acute angle.

stem: An angle measures $90°$. What type of angle is this?
An angle measures $145°$. What type of angle is this?
hint: An obtuse angle is between $90°$ and $180°$.
explanation: $145°$ is between $90°$ and $180°$, so it is an obtuse angle.

stem: An angle measures $220°$. What type of angle is this?
hint: A reflex angle is between $180°$ and $360°$.
explanation: $220°$ is between $180°$ and $360°$, so it is a reflex angle.

stem: Two angles are supplementary. One angle is $67°$. Find the other angle in degrees.
Two angles are complementary. One angle is $38°$. Find the other angle in degrees.
Two straight lines intersect at a point. One of the angles formed is $74°$. Find the size of the vertically opposite angle in degrees.
explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal, so the angle is also $74°$.

stem: Four angles meet at a point. Three of the angles are $85°$, $120°$ and $90°$. Find the fourth angle $x°$.
hint: Angles at a point add to $360°$.

stem: Three angles on a straight line are $x°$, $2x°$ and $45°$. Find the value of $x$.
hint: Angles on a straight line add to $180°$.

stem: Two straight lines intersect. One angle is $(3x + 10)°$ and the vertically opposite angle is $(5x - 20)°$. Find the value of $x$.
Two supplementary angles are in the ratio $2:3$. Find the size of the larger angle in degrees.
hint: Let the angles be $2k$ and $3k$. They add to $180°$.

stem: Three angles on a straight line are $x°$, $(x + 30)°$ and $(2x - 10)°$. Find the value of $x$.
hint: Angles on a straight line add to $180°$.
